rationale: In the conceptual-development section on purchase timing, the authors explicitly summarize Pocheptsova et al. (2009) and use its depletion result to justify a directional prediction for their business-hours variable. This is more than a passing background mention because the cited finding is linked to a specific hypothesis about choosing higher-priced/higher-quality options. At the same time, the citing paper does not replicate or refine the depletion experiments directly, so the role is best viewed as applying the prior idea in a new field context.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Pocheptsova et al. (2009) as theoretical support that depletion can shift choice toward more expensive options, then applies that logic to hotel booking by treating business-hour purchases as a depletion-related circumstance. This applied framing helps motivate predictions for quality choice (and related satisfaction patterns) in their two-stage model.
